sourceMovie.title: Prime
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Prime for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ben Younger keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: (500) Days of Summer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Prime clicked for you, (500) Days of Summer (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Prime?
faq.answer: Try (500) Days of Summer, Annie Hall, The Apartment—each shares DNA with Prime while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Prime?
faq.answer: Prime (2005) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Prime worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
